item.title: Morning Bid: Stocks rise, blockade holds, talks may resume
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Gregor Stuart Hunter

item.title: Uber commits $10 billion to robotaxis in strategy shift, FT says
item.description: Uber has committed more than $10 ​billion to buying thousands of autonomous vehicles ‌and taking stakes in their developers, breaking from its asset-light "gig economy" business model to avoid disruption from robotaxis, ​the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Federal agencies skirt Trump’s Anthropic ban to test its advanced AI model, Politico reports
item.description: Federal agencies and ​government officials are ‌quietly sidestepping U.S. President ​Donald Trump’s ​ban on working ⁠with Anthropic, ​Politico reported ​on Tuesday.

item.title: Korean AI chip startup DEEPX, Hyundai work on robots powered by generative AI
item.description: South Korean AI chip startup DEEPX will expand its partnership with Hyundai Motor Group to develop a computing platform for generative AI robots using its ​second generation of low-power chips, its top executive said, as it gets set ‌for an IPO.

item.title: Asian shares scale six-week peak on hopes for US-Iran peace talks
item.description: Asian stocks tracked Wall Street ​higher on Wednesday as hopes for a resumption of U.S.-Iran peace talks pushed oil ‌prices lower, while the dollar steadied after seven days of losses.

item.title: Former US Treasury Secretary Yellen says one Fed rate cut possible this year
item.description: Former U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen sees one interest rate cut by ​the Federal Reserve as possible this year, even as the Iran war creates supply ‌shocks in the global economy that put pressure on inflation.

item.title: Safe-haven dollar near six-week lows on hopes of fresh Iran talks
item.description: The U.S. ​dollar lingered near six-week lows on Wednesday, surrendering nearly all the gains it ‌had made since the Middle East war erupted as hopeful signs of another round of talks between the U.S. and Iran lifted risk appetite.

item.title: Iran war promises green edge for Asia as plastic packaging runs short
item.description: An 'eco-friendly' range of paper tubes and pouches touted by Yonwoo, a South Korean maker ​of packaging for cosmetics, has reaped unexpected benefit from the Iran war, which has disrupted supplies of the plastic needed to turn out single-use ‌wrapping.

item.title: Oil prices fall for a second day on expectations US-Iran talks may resume
item.description: Oil prices fell for a second day ‌on Wednesday on expectations peace talks between the U.S. and Iran may resume and eventually release supply from the key Middle East producing region trapped by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Anthropic draws VC interest at up to $800 billion valuation, Business Insider reports
item.description: Anthropic has received multiple offers from ​venture capital firms in recent weeks to invest ‌in the Claude maker at valuations as high as $800 billion, more than double its current value, Business Insider reported ​on Tuesday, citing sources.

item.title: Remote working, prices drive US home coffee consumption to highest in 14 years
item.description: Remote working and the need to save money are leading to an increase in coffee consumption at ​home in the United States, in a reversal of a ‌pre-pandemic trend of increasing consumption at cafes, according to industry data and analysts.

item.title: China's Huaqin seeks to raise nearly $581 million in HK listing amid market volatility
item.description: China-based technology firm Huaqin launched a Hong Kong ​share sale on Wednesday, aiming to raise up ‌to HK$4.55 billion ($580.85 million), as the city's capital markets continue to attract interest despite war-driven market volatility.

item.title: Quadra Capital nears deal to buy $3 billion of assets from Banco de Brasília, sources say
item.description: Brazilian independent asset manager Quadra Capital is nearing the acquisition of 15 ‌billion reais ($2.99 billion) of assets from state-owned Banco de Brasília (BRB) that were previously held by liquidated lender Banco Master, said three people familiar with the talks.

item.title: Treasury chief says US growth may exceed 3% or 3.5% this year despite Iran war
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Tuesday the underlying U.S. ​economy remained strong and that growth could still ‌exceed 3% or 3.5% this year despite the impact of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Maine legislature approves first US moratorium on big data centers
item.description: Maine lawmakers have passed a bill that could make it the first ​U.S. state to put a moratorium on new data centers as ‌opposition to the electricity-hungry facilities grows across the country over their impact on household energy bills and the environment.

item.title: Google sued by rival app store Aptoide over alleged monopoly
item.description: A new antitrust lawsuit on Tuesday accuses Google of shutting out rival Android app stores by monopolizing app distribution ​and billing, violating U.S. antitrust law.

item.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.4-Cyber a week after rival's announcement of AI model
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday unveiled GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of its latest flagship model fine-tuned specifically ​for defensive cybersecurity work, following rival Anthropic's ‌announcement of frontier AI model Mythos.

item.title: Meta inks deal with Broadcom for custom AI chips
item.description: Meta will work with chip designer Broadcom to produce several generations of custom artificial intelligence processors under ​an expanded deal as the social media giant races to build out ‌the computing capacity needed to power AI features across its apps.

item.title: Volkswagen faces Q1 charge after halting EV production in Tennessee, analysts say
item.description: Volkswagen is expected to take a hit to first-quarter earnings from ​the decision to end production of ‌its ID.4 electric SUV at the German carmaker's Tennessee plant, analysts said on Tuesday ​after a call with management.

item.title: Trading Day: Wall Street jumps on optimism over revived US-Iran talks, focus on earnings
item.description: Stocks rallied and crude fell on Tuesday as the promise of ongoing talks between the United States and Iran boosted hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East, while corporate earnings took center stage.

item.title: Brazilian steelmaker CSN pushes ahead with cement unit sale
item.description: Brazilian steelmaker CSN is pushing ahead with the sale of its cement unit, which could ​fetch over 10 billion reais ($2 billion), according to two people ‌familiar with the matter.

item.title: Goldman Sachs files for its first bitcoin ETF product
item.description: Goldman Sachs' asset management division plans to launch its first cryptocurrency exchange-traded ‌fund in the coming months, according to a Tuesday filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

item.title: NAACP sues Musk's xAI, alleging illegal operation of gas turbines
item.description: The largest U.S. civil rights group on Tuesday sued xAI and a subsidiary, claiming they illegally operated more than two dozen gas turbines ​in Mississippi to power its Colossus 2 data center, posing a ‌health risk to local residents.

item.title: IFC, Citigroup sign $98 million local currency borrowing facility for South Africa
item.description: The World Bank Group’s private-sector arm, the International Finance Corporation, and U.S. bank Citigroup have signed a ​new 1.6 billion rand ($98 million) borrowing facility aimed at expanding ‌local-currency financing in South Africa, the IFC and Citigroup said on Tuesday.

item.title: US issues license allowing transactions with some Venezuelan banks
item.description: The Trump administration issued ‌two new Venezuela-related general licenses on Tuesday, ​including one that ​allows financial transactions involving certain ⁠Venezuelan banks and ​Venezuelan government individuals, according ​to documents posted to the U.S. Treasury Department's website.

item.title: Wall Street monitors private credit risk as AI disruption, outflows cause concern
item.description: Wall Street executives said they were stress‑testing or monitoring private credit portfolios as the asset class comes under scrutiny, but said they were comfortable with their exposure.

item.title: United's chief takes fight with American to White House with merger pitch
item.description: For more than a decade, United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby has argued that the U.S. airline industry can support only two true global premium carriers. Now, he has taken that view to the White House — floating the idea of a merger ​with his fiercest rival, American Airlines .

item.title: Bessent disparages IMF, World Bank forecasts, says US will quickly cycle through higher prices
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Tuesday took issue ​with cuts in global growth forecasts and higher inflation projections announced by the International Monetary ‌Fund and World Bank as a result of the war in the Middle East, saying the institutions had probably "overreacted."

item.title: BoE's Bailey sees major cybersecurity risks in new Anthropic model
item.description: Central banks and financial regulators must quickly understand the implications of a new artificial intelligence model that could pose major cybersecurity dangers, Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey ​said on Tuesday.

item.title: US set to launch tariff refund system on April 20
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration plans to launch next Monday the system it will use for ​issuing refunds to American importers for $166 billion the companies paid in tariffs that ‌the U.S. Supreme Court struck down in February as unlawful.
